title: Spec: Connector Credentials Documentation
description:
Create comprehensive credential documentation for all 24 glovebox connectors.

## Goal
Operators deploying glovebox connectors need clear guidance on:
1. What credentials each connector requires
2. Where to generate those credentials
3. What permissions/scopes to request
4. What data each permission grants access to

## Output
Single reference document at docs/connector-credentials.md

## Format
Alphabetical by provider/ecosystem, with each connector section containing:
- Environment variables table
- Auth type
- Setup steps (2-5 bullets)
- Permissions-to-data mapping table
- Links to official provider documentation
design:
# Connector Credentials Reference - Design Spec

## Document Location
`docs/connector-credentials.md`

## Document Structure

### 1. Header & Overview
- Brief intro explaining what this doc covers
- Quick-reference table of all 24 connectors with auth type at a glance
- Links to deployment.md and connector-guide.md

### 2. Per-Connector Section Template

Each connector section follows this structure:

```markdown
## Provider Name

### Connector Name

| Environment Variable | Required | Description |
|---------------------|----------|-------------|
| `VAR_NAME` | Yes/No | What it is |

**Auth Type:** PAT / OAuth2 / API Key / App Password / None

**Setup:**
1. Go to [Provider Developer Portal](link)
2. Create X
3. Grant Y permissions
4. Copy Z to environment variable

**Permissions & Data Access:**
| Permission/Scope | Grants Access To |
|-----------------|------------------|
| `scope.name` | What data this unlocks in glovebox |

**Official Documentation:**
- [Auth Guide](link)
- [Scopes Reference](link)
```

### 3. Provider Groupings (Alphabetical)

1. **arXiv** - No auth required
2. **Atlassian**
   - Jira (API token + email, or OAuth2 3LO)
   - Trello (API key + token)
3. **Bluesky** - App password (OAuth coming)
4. **GitHub** - Fine-grained PAT (recommended) or classic PAT
5. **GitLab** - Personal access token or OAuth2
6. **Google**
   - Gmail (OAuth2, gmail.readonly scope)
   - Google Calendar (OAuth2)
   - Google Drive (OAuth2)
   - YouTube (OAuth2 or API key for public data)
7. **Hacker News** - No auth required
8. **IMAP** - Username/password or XOAUTH2 (provider-specific notes)
9. **LinkedIn** - OAuth2 3LO (restricted scopes need partner approval)
10. **Meta**
    - Facebook (OAuth2, Business/Creator accounts only)
    - Instagram (OAuth2, Business/Creator accounts only)
11. **Microsoft**
    - OneDrive (Microsoft Graph OAuth2)
    - Outlook (Microsoft Graph OAuth2)
    - Teams (Microsoft Graph OAuth2)
12. **Notion** - Internal integration token or OAuth2
13. **RSS** - No auth required
14. **Semantic Scholar** - No auth required (optional API key for higher rate limits)
15. **Steam** - Web API key
16. **X (Twitter)** - Bearer token (app-only) or OAuth2 PKCE (user context)

### 4. Data Access Mapping Examples

**GitHub (Fine-Grained PAT):**
| Permission | Content Staged |
|------------|---------------|
| `contents:read` | Repository files, commits |
| `issues:read` | Issue bodies, comments |
| `pull_requests:read` | PR descriptions, reviews |
| `metadata:read` | Repo metadata (required) |

**Gmail:**
| Scope | Content Staged |
|-------|---------------|
| `gmail.readonly` | Email messages, attachments |
| `gmail.metadata` | Headers only (sender, subject, date) |

**Microsoft Graph:**
| Scope | Content Staged |
|-------|---------------|
| `Mail.Read` | Outlook messages |
| `Files.Read` | OneDrive files |
| `Calendars.Read` | Calendar events |
| `ChannelMessage.Read.All` | Teams messages |

### 5. Special Sections

**OAuth Connectors:**
- Note on refresh token handling
- Link to RefreshableTokenSource in connector-guide.md
- Token file location ($GLOVEBOX_STATE_DIR/token.json)

**Webhook-Enabled Connectors (GitHub, Meta, X):**
- Separate subsection for webhook secret setup
- HMAC verification requirements
- Endpoint URL format

**No-Auth Connectors:**
- Note that no credentials needed
- Mention any rate limits or fair-use policies

### 6. Security Notes Section

Final section covering:
- Never commit credentials to git
- Use K8s secrets, 1Password, Vault, etc.
- Principle of least privilege
- Token rotation recommendations by provider:
  - GitHub: No expiry by default, recommend 90-day rotation
  - Google: Refresh tokens can be revoked; rotate client secrets annually
  - Microsoft: Tokens expire per policy; monitor Entra ID
  - GitLab: Max 400 days, forced rotation

### 7. Official Documentation Links

Each provider section MUST include links to authoritative docs:

| Provider | Auth Docs |
|----------|-----------|
| GitHub | https://docs.github.com/en/rest/authentication/authenticating-to-the-rest-api |
| Google | https://developers.google.com/identity/protocols/oauth2 |
| Gmail | https://developers.google.com/workspace/gmail/api/auth/scopes |
| Jira | https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/basic-auth-for-rest-apis/ |
| Trello | https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/trello/guides/rest-api/authorization/ |
| Bluesky | https://docs.bsky.app/docs/advanced-guides/api-directory |
| LinkedIn | https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/shared/authentication/authentication |
| X | https://developer.x.com/en/docs/authentication/oauth-2-0 |
| Meta | https://developers.facebook.com/docs/facebook-login/guides/access-tokens/ |
| Steam | https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/webapi_overview/auth |
| GitLab | https://docs.gitlab.com/api/rest/authentication/ |
| YouTube | https://developers.google.com/youtube/v3/guides/authentication |
| Microsoft | https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/auth/auth-concepts |
| Notion | https://developers.notion.com/docs/authorization |

## Implementation Notes

### Research Required Per Connector
For each connector, implementation requires:
1. Reading main.go to identify env vars
2. Reading connector.go to identify auth method used
3. Cross-referencing with official API docs
4. Documenting permission-to-data mapping

### Connectors Requiring Special Attention

**IMAP:** Provider-specific guidance needed:
- Gmail: XOAUTH2 only (basic auth dead)
- Microsoft 365: XOAUTH2 required as of April 2026
- Fastmail: App passwords still work
- Self-hosted: Varies

**Microsoft connectors (OneDrive, Outlook, Teams):** 
- Currently in connectors/ but may not be fully implemented
- Verify actual env vars used before documenting

**Meta connector:**
- Instagram Basic Display API deprecated Dec 2024
- Only Business/Creator accounts supported
- Document the Facebook Business Login flow
notes:
## Self-Review Corrections (2026-04-01)

### Connector Count
- Actual count: 22 connectors (not 24)
- Full list: arxiv, bluesky, gcalendar, gdrive, github, gitlab, gmail, hackernews, imap, jira, linkedin, meta, notion, onedrive, outlook, rss, semantic-scholar, steam, teams, trello, x, youtube

### Env Var Corrections from Code Review

| Connector | Env Vars |
|-----------|----------|
| GitHub | GITHUB_TOKEN (required), webhook_secret_env in config |
| Gmail | GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET (OAuth2) |
| Jira | JIRA_EMAIL, JIRA_API_TOKEN |
| Trello | TRELLO_API_KEY, TRELLO_TOKEN |
| Bluesky | BLUESKY_IDENTIFIER, BLUESKY_APP_PASSWORD |
| LinkedIn | LINKEDIN_TOKEN, LINKEDIN_PERSON_ID |
| Meta | META_ACCESS_TOKEN (required), META_APP_SECRET (optional), META_VERIFY_TOKEN (optional) |
| X | X_BEARER_TOKEN (required), X_WEBHOOK_SECRET (optional) |
| Steam | STEAM_API_KEY (optional for some endpoints) |
| Notion | NOTION_TOKEN |
| IMAP | IMAP_HOST, IMAP_USERNAME, IMAP_PASSWORD (required); IMAP_PORT, IMAP_TLS (optional) |
| Microsoft (OneDrive/Outlook/Teams) | MS_CLIENT_ID, MS_CLIENT_SECRET, MS_TENANT_ID |

### Provider Grouping Update
- Meta is ONE connector (uses Facebook Graph API), not separate FB + Instagram
- Remove separate Facebook/Instagram entries; document as "Meta" with note that it accesses Facebook Graph API

### Currency Notes
- IMAP: Code uses basic auth (Login). No XOAUTH2 support yet.
- Microsoft basic auth deprecation April 2026 affects IMAP connector if pointed at M365
- Bluesky uses app passwords; OAuth support not yet implemented in connector
